codeReview.summary: I reviewed the fix feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit diff (`69128e2`), plus the prior failed feature diffs (`60438bb`, `fdd7e9d`) and prior review reports. The fix now closes both blocking gaps: `test_invariants` has a layered orphan-leak assertion (named process scan + HOME-descendant scan + tmpdir FD scan, with live mitmdump PID exclusion) and `test_nav_keyboard` now parses runtime input via `RuntimeLog` with caret-notation decoding and unit coverage for both raw and caret mouse sequences.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared testing docs still describe only `script -q /dev/null` PTY wrapping and do not document `script -k` runtime-input encoding (`^[` caret notation), which was central to this failure class.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/user-testing.md wrapper guidance shows `script -q /dev/null ...` without runtime-input/caret decoding notes, while this fix introduces `client/tests/tuistory/runtime_log.py` to decode caret-notation control sequences before mouse-event assertions.

summary: Pass: this fix adequately addresses the two requested prior failures by hardening harness orphan-scope invariants and making runtime mouse-event detection robust to caret-encoded `script -k` logs, with direct tests for each leak mode and encoding form.
